nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Singleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Singleton (Lancashire), probably from Old English scingol ‘shingle, wooden roof tile’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Bearers of this surname are said to be descended from Ughtred de Sinleton, holder of lands in Amounderness wapentake (Lancashire) c. 1160–90. This name was first recorded in Ireland in 1308 and firmly established in Louth and Monaghan during the 17th century.
habitational name from Singleton (Sussex), probably from Old English sængel, sengel ‘bundle, tuft’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Shingleton
meanings: English: late medieval variant of   Singleton  .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton was born about 1158, in Singleton, Lancashire, England as the daughter of Ughtred of Singleton. She had at least 6 sons with Adam de Kellet II. She died in 1219, in Lancashire, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton est née environ 1158, à Singleton, Lancashire, Angleterre, fille de Ughtred of Singleton. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils avec Adam de Kellet II. Elle est décédée en 1219, à Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton wurde um 1158 in Singleton, Lancashire, England, als Tochter von Ughtred of Singleton geboren. Sie hatte mit Adam de Kellet II mindestens 6 Söhne. Sie starb 1219 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton è nata il circa 1158 nel Singleton, Lancashire, England come figlia di Ughtred of Singleton. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli con Adam de Kellet II. Morì nel 1219, nel Lancashire, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singletonは約1158年にイギリスランカシュアイアシングレトンでUghtred of Singletonの娘として生まれた。彼女はAdam de Kellet IIとの間に少なくとも6人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1219年にイギリスランカシュアイアで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton(은)는 대략 1158년 영국 랭카실르 징글레턴에서 Ughtred of Singleton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Adam de Kellet II(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1219년 영국 랭카실르에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton nasceu aproximadamente 1158, em Singleton, Lancashire, Inglaterra filha de Ughtred of Singleton. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos com Adam de Kellet II. Ela faleceu em 1219, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton родилась около 1158 г. в местности синглетон, ланкашир, Англия. Её отца звали Ughtred of Singleton. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей от Adam de Kellet II. Она умерла в 1219 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Matilda of Singleton nació aproximadamente 1158, en Singleton, Lancashire, Inglaterra como hija de Ughtred of Singleton. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos con Adam de Kellet II. Murió en 1219, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
